_meta.keyKnowledge: We experience calming strategies that help us feel better when we are upset.

_source.w: We experience calming strategies that help us feel better when we are upset.
y: Learning to calm down with help is the first step in managing emotions.
Feeling supported when we are upset helps us feel safe.
These calming experiences teach us that big feelings can be managed.
eg: We calm down when an adult rocks, pats or holds us gently.
We use a sensory toy or weighted blanket to help us feel calm.
We listen to soothing music when we are feeling upset.
We move to a quiet space with an adult when we need to settle.
We watch calming visuals — like bubbles or a lava lamp — to regulate.
We feel better after deep pressure or a gentle squeeze.
